---
title: "About GEMMA"
---

## Background

The GEMMA project (Greenhouse gas Measurement and Modelling Advancement) was
established to address a fundamental challenge in climate science and government policy: how can we ensure that national greenhouse gas emissions emissions estimates are accurate, timely, and credible?

GEMMA develops the science and tools needed to
independently evaluate emissions inventories using atmospheric observations and
advanced inverse modelling techniques. It is designed as a prototype for a future near-real-time operational system that could be used by governments to verify their reported emissions and track progress towards climate targets.

## Objectives

The project has four principal objectives:

1. **Advance measurement capability** – develop and deploy next-generation
   instruments for precise, traceable greenhouse gas measurements across the UK.

2. **Improve atmospheric inversion methods** – refine the statistical and
   computational techniques used to infer surface emissions from atmospheric
   concentration data.

3. **Evaluate national inventories** – produce independent, observation-based
   estimates of UK greenhouse gas emissions and compare these with official
   reported figures.

4. **Inform policy** – translate scientific findings into accessible, actionable
   outputs for government, regulators, and international frameworks such as the
   Global Stocktake under the Paris Agreement.

## Gases and sectors covered

GEMMA focuses on the key greenhouse gases regulated under the Kyoto Protocol
and Paris Agreement:

| Gas | Main sources covered |
|---|---|
| Carbon dioxide (CO₂) | Fossil fuel combustion, land use |
| Methane (CH₄) | Agriculture, waste, oil and gas |
| Nitrous oxide (N₂O) | Agriculture, industry |
| Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) | Refrigeration, air conditioning |
| Perfluorocarbons (PFCs) | Semiconductor manufacture, aluminium |
| Sulphur hexafluoride (SF₆) | Electrical switchgear |

## Consortium

GEMMA is a collaboration between:

**National Physical Laboratory (NPL)**
Lead partner. NPL provides world-class measurement science and uncertainty quantification expertise and
is developing primary gas standards.

**University of Bristol**
Atmospheric chemistry and inverse modelling. Bristol leads the UK Deriving Emissions linked to Climate Change (DECC) network and is a co-founder of the Advanced Global Atmospheric Gases Experiment (AGAGE).

**Met Office**
Inverse modelling and stakeholder engagement. As a co-founder of the DECC network, the Met Office provides NAME dispersion model simulations and inverse model estimates via the Inversion Technique for Emissions Modelling (InTEM). Their operational forecasting and climate modelling expertise supports GEMMA's long-term vision for a near-real-time operational system.

**University of East Anglia (UEA)**
Atmospheric observations and data analysis. UEA operates the Weybourne
Atmospheric Observatory monitoring network, an international renowned measurement station that is part of the European Integrated Carbon Observation System (ICOS).

**University of Manchester**
Atmospheric measurement and source attribution. Manchester contributes
expertise in greenhouse gas measurement techniques and emissions analysis.

**National Centre for Atmospheric Science (NCAS)**
National research infrastructure for atmospheric science. NCAS provides
shared facilities, long-term observational datasets, and expertise that
underpin GEMMA's measurement and modelling activities.

**National Centre for Earth Observation (NCEO)**
Earth observation science and data analysis. NCEO contributes ground-based remote sensing and satellite retrieval expertise to complement GEMMA's ground-based network.

## Funding

GEMMA is funded by [UKRI](https://www.ukri.org) through the Building a Green Future programme. The project runs from 2024 to 2027.

## Contact

For general enquiries about the project, please contact
GEMMA Charlotte Massey at NPL.
